title: Ep16 Newsclips after Yuis death requesting translations
display_title: Ep16 Newsclips after Yuis death requesting translations
wikitext:
[]: Ep16 Newsclips after Yui's 'death' (requesting translations)             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Sep 20, 2011 10:36 am These images flash by as Shinji hears unidentified voices speculate about Gendo killing his own wife. I append them with translation/summations that appeared on ADV's Platinum release. However not every one of them even received that it seems. There are many more in the whole sequence before these; I chose to focus on those from after the year 2004. I omitted some that were translated simply as 'Fear', 'Mistake', 'Bribery', and 'Director Gendo Ikari' for brevity's sake and because they appeared to be simple enough (often one or two characters). I didn't see discussion or analysis of these anywhere in casual searching, so I was wondering if any with Japanese knowledge would like to take more thorough and perhaps more enlightening cracks at translating them? Anyone wish to speculate what the 'Supreme Court Finds Not Guilty' stuff is about? Is it about Gendo and a bribery allegation, or about Gendo and a wrongful death allegation about Yui? I've always found it fascinating that Shinji thinks about him being responsible for her death deep in his mind, even thinking outright that "He killed mother!" in episode 20's 'Enemy' sequence. Anyway, without further ado: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Ep16 Newsclips after Yui's 'death' (requesting translati             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Sep 20, 2011 11:35 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: These images flash by as Shinji hears unidentified voices speculate about Gendo killing his own wife.

[]: Re: Ep16 Newsclips after Yui's 'death' (requesting translati             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Sep 20, 2011 1:16 pm Thanks for your translation help with this, symbv :) BTW, to answer your question: symbv wrote: I wonder where the "Bribery" bit came from? Surely it did not come up in the images quoted.... [...] > (not shown: "Bribery", "Director Gendo Ikari") Where are the words of Bribery and Gendo? The big kanji in this pic only said "genome biology".

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Sep 20, 2011 6:25 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Sep 20, 2011 7:08 pm Creative writing eh? So when Misato hacks the "History of Gainax" in EOE it wasn't the first time they had done this. [wikimg width=500]File:M25 C058 gainax.jpg[/wikimg] "The Blind Men and the Elephant" goes way back; here's a version with 6 blind men: [url]http://en.wikisource.org/wiki/The_poems_of_John_Godfrey_Saxe/The_Blind_Men_and_the_Elephant[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Sep 20, 2011 9:32 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: "Bribery"

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Sep 21, 2011 4:19 pm I heard it as six blind mice; our teacher read it to us in first grade.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Sep 24, 2011 8:15 am Ah, finally someone gives these ever so mysterious sequences attention. They're often overlooked if you remember that they detail the mysterious past of the main character . the basic story "Dude is depressed after wife dies and disposes of kid" sounds pretty simple, but if you look closely, there are a lot of half-mentioned, mystery-shrouded events in the backstory of the Ikaris. Think of Episode 20's "It was you who ran away". I suspect that little Shinji overheard ppl talking about this, and, being about three, didn't quite get the complexety of the situation and promptly rejected or accused his already very lost father. Of course, a "professional" parent should be above this, since the kid doesn't quite grasp the whole thing yet and will probably not even remember most of it, but Gendo being Gendo and already having to cope with the loss of his wife, was probably hopelessly overburdened by that. Must be horrible - first,your wife dies of all sudden in front of your eyes, and then, ppl accuse you of murdering her! Not to mention that it was Yui herself who built the EVAs in the first place, and of course, she volunteered for the test, so Gendo was really innocent here for once. But of course, media wants a scapegoat. What astonishes me is that the story even made it into the newspapers, you'd assume they could cover up one person dying since they were apparently liquidating ppl left and right. The "bribery" in question probably refers to SEELE getting the authorities to drop the case with the power of some himalaya-sized piles of Dollars, Yens and Euros. So they could keep building Mechas in peace.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Sep 24, 2011 8:45 am Kendrix wrote: The "bribery" in question probably refers to SEELE getting the authorities to drop the case with the power of some himalaya-sized piles of Dollars, Yens and Euros. So they could keep building Mechas in peace.

Must be horrible - first,your wife dies of all sudden in front of your eyes, and then, ppl accuse you of murdering her!
